mechanism_narrative: A downward turn in FEDFUNDS lowers short-rate discounting and signals easier financial conditions, which lifts equity multiples and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of periods when FEDFUNDS is falling over a 3-month window, or if Rank IC stays below 0.03 in any 3-year rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates while FEDFUNDS does not rise enough to offset it, real policy turns more accommodative and equity discount rates fall, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation rises above FEDFUNDS by at least 1.0 percentage point
failure_condition: Reject if the interaction of CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS does not outperform either series alone, or if triggered episodes produce negative 20-day SPX returns more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE from a low starting level marks weakening household income and demand, which should depress near-term risk appetite and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 60% of periods after a 0.3 pp 3-month UNRATE rise, or if the signal's rolling hit rate falls below 45% for two consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: Employment gains in PAYEMS combined with stable UNRATE indicate broad labor-market resilience, supporting earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases for 3 consecutive months while UNRATE does not rise
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-UNRATE condition does not yield positive 60-day SPX returns in at least 55% of cases, or if the combined signal is no better than a PAYEMS-only rule.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in INDPRO captures improving industrial activity and inventory restocking, which should translate into stronger cyclical earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if positive month-over-month INDPRO changes fail to coincide with positive 20-day SPX returns more than 50% of the time, or if the Rank IC is statistically indistinguishable from zero over any 24-month window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in HOUST signals a housing slowdown that typically spills into construction, consumption, and credit demand, which should pressure equities and lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls by more than 8% year over year
failure_condition: Reject if triggered housing contractions are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 55% of instances, or if the signal loses sign consistency across two consecutive recession-risk episodes.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS growth slows while GDP growth is also weak, the combination indicates soft final demand and weaker corporate revenue momentum, which should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month growth turns negative and GDPC1 annualized quarterly growth is below 2%
failure_condition: Reject if the combined RSAFS-GDPC1 slowdown condition does not precede negative 60-day SPX returns in at least 55% of occurrences, or if either variable alone explains the same effect as well as the pair.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening cycle, a high FEDFUNDS rate together with elevated CPIAUCSL means policy is restrictive relative to inflation, compressing valuation multiples and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5% and CPIAUCSL inflation is above 3%
failure_condition: Reject if the joint high-rate/high-inflation state fails to produce negative 5-day SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or if the effect disappears once FEDFUNDS is conditioned on CPIAUCSL.
